raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Corporate landlords are purchasing entire neighborhoods of single-family homes, impacting working families' homeownership opportunities.",
    "Wall Street-backed corporations are reaping rental profits at the expense of everyday Californians' chances to buy starter homes.",
    "A bill has been introduced to prevent institutional investors owning more than 1,000 single-family homes from purchasing additional properties for rental conversion.",
    "Metro regions that were previously affordable for first-time homebuyers are now targeted by wealthy Wall Street firms due to chronic housing shortages.",
    "Institutional investors began amassing portfolios of single-family homes and converting them into rentals following the 2007-2009 financial crisis.",
    "Homeownership rates in California have declined over the years, with only 55% of homes owned by occupants in 2022, down from about 60% in 2006.",
    "The homeownership rate for Californians aged 35 to 45 declined from about 49% to nearly 40% between 2000 to 2021."
  ]
}
```
